sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content: Surgery is the primary treatment for localized vulvar cancer (SCC, adenocarcinoma, and melanoma) with the goal of achieving negative margins while preserving function and minimizing morbidity.

cards.name: Simple partial vulvectomy
cards.detail: Approach: Local excision with 1- to 2-cm margins; Indications: Stage IA SCC/adenocarcinoma with ≤1 mm invasion; Key complications: Wound infection, dehiscence, pain; Operative mortality: Low (<1%)

cards.name: Radical partial vulvectomy
cards.detail: Approach: Excision of tumor with wide margins and deep tissue to fascia; Indications: Stage IB or select Stage II SCC/adenocarcinoma; Key complications: Wound complications, lymphedema, sexual dysfunction; Operative mortality: Low (<1%)

cards.name: Radical total vulvectomy
cards.detail: Approach: Excision of entire vulva with wide margins; Indications: Extensive disease or multifocal lesions; Key complications: Significant wound morbidity, lymphedema, psychosexual impact; Operative mortality: Low (<1%)

cards.name: Inguinofemoral lymphadenectomy
cards.detail: Approach: Removal of superficial inguinal and deep femoral lymph nodes via separate incision; Indications: Clinically node-positive disease or SLNB positive with macrometastases; Key complications: Lymphedema (30%–70%), wound complications (20%–40%), infection; Operative mortality: Low (<1%)

cards.name: Sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB)
cards.detail: Approach: Identification and removal of sentinel nodes using radiocolloid and blue dye; Indications: Clinically node-negative, unifocal tumor <4 cm; Key complications: False-negative rate (6.4%), lymphedema (lower than lymphadenectomy); Operative mortality: Minimal

cards.name: Pelvic exenteration
cards.detail: Approach: Radical resection of pelvic organs; Indications: Central recurrence after prior radiation; Key complications: High morbidity, urinary diversion, colostomy; Operative mortality: Higher (3%–5%)
